customer_anxiety_profile: Low anxiety, high busyness. Quincy is warm and self-directing but genuinely time-constrained — three businesses, four kids, coaches three sports. He will not chase the appointment if it becomes inconvenient. His wife is the financial decision-maker and is not on the calendar.
unresolved_concerns: Wife (financial decision-maker, bill-holder, inquiry initiator) is not included in the appointment — closer is presenting to the wrong person.
Utility bill was not collected. Quincy texted his wife mid-call but no confirmation that the bill was sent to Parker before the appointment.
Appointment format may be wrong — Quincy signaled phone-call evenings would be more practical than in-person Friday morning, but in-person Friday 10AM was booked.
One-hour block was NOT set — Parker told Quincy it usually takes 20-30 minutes, which may reduce perceived commitment to the appointment.
sensitive_topics_raised: Wife just opened a new retail business last week — she is in a high-stress launch period and may be difficult to reach or include.
Quincy described his 14-year head shop as exhausting ('wake up breathing the shit, go to sleep thinking about it') — he is open to financial relief but not to adding complexity.
exact_promises_rep_made: Parker will text Quincy at 815-322-XXXX after the call.
Appointment is booked for Friday at 10AM, in-person.
closer_should_know: The wife initiated this inquiry and handles all household finances — Quincy said so explicitly. She is not on the appointment. The closer's first move should be to confirm whether she can join, either by phone or in person. Presenting without her is presenting to the wrong decision-maker.
